nodes.label: Matter and Materials
nodes.description: Everything around us is made of matter in three states

nodes.label: Solids
nodes.description: Matter with a fixed shape and fixed size

nodes.label: Fixed Shape
nodes.description: Solids keep their shape unless you push, bend, or break them

nodes.label: Tightly Packed Particles
nodes.description: Particles in solids are very close together and only vibrate in place

nodes.label: Examples
nodes.description: Ice cube, book, stone, chair, pencil, wooden block

nodes.label: Liquids
nodes.description: Matter that flows and takes the shape of its container

nodes.label: Can Flow
nodes.description: Liquids pour and move from one place to another

nodes.label: No Fixed Shape
nodes.description: Takes the shape of the glass, bowl, or bottle it is poured into

nodes.label: Examples
nodes.description: Water, milk, juice, oil, honey, soup

nodes.label: Gases
nodes.description: Matter with no fixed shape and no fixed size, fills all available space

nodes.label: Usually Invisible
nodes.description: Most gases cannot be seen but can sometimes be felt or smelled

nodes.label: Spreads Everywhere
nodes.description: Gas particles move fast and fill all the space in a room or container

nodes.label: Examples
nodes.description: Air, oxygen, carbon dioxide, helium in balloons, water vapour

nodes.label: Changing States
nodes.description: Matter can change from one state to another by heating or cooling

nodes.label: Melting & Freezing
nodes.description: Heating melts solid to liquid (ice to water); cooling freezes liquid to solid (water to ice)

nodes.label: Evaporation & Condensation
nodes.description: Heating turns liquid to gas (boiling water to steam); cooling turns gas to liquid (steam to water drops)
